session.title: Jenn's Ventures AI Night
session.subtitle: Venturer learner workbook flow
session.notes: Pre-populated from the current repo direction: guided start, prompt improvement, reflection, branching, independent build, and close.

steps.title: Welcome and what tonight is for
steps.goal: Set expectations, lower pressure, explain that the goal is learning how output changes when prompts change.
steps.learner_prompt: Write one thing you hope AI could help you do better by the end of tonight.
steps.facilitator_notes: Keep this calm and short. Emphasize that surprising results and weak results are both useful for learning.

steps.title: Live demo: weak prompt vs stronger prompt
steps.starter_prompt: Plan something fun for this weekend.
steps.improved_prompt: Plan a 2-hour low-cost weekend outing for two teens in Kitchener with one indoor backup. Keep it under $25 and show it as a simple timeline.
steps.reflection_question: What changed between the first result and the second result?
steps.facilitator_notes: Use this to anchor the whole night. Show how audience, constraints, budget, and output format matter.

steps.title: Guided exercise: fix a weak prompt
steps.goal: Give every learner one successful prompt-improvement loop early in the session.

activity_cards.weak_prompt: Plan something fun for this weekend.
activity_cards.better_prompt_hint: Add audience, budget, place, backup plan, and output format.
Add audience, tone, length, and what must stay clear.
Add grade level, what confuses you, how much time you have, and what format would help.

steps.learner_prompt: Pick one card. Improve the prompt. Paste the result. Note what changed.
steps.reflection_question: What did you expect the better prompt to return, and what actually happened?

steps.title: Expectation versus result
steps.goal: Teach that a polished answer is not automatically a correct or complete answer.
steps.learner_prompt: Record: what you expected, what the tool returned, what gap remained, and what you would verify next.
steps.facilitator_notes: This is the bridge from 'cool output' to real thinking.

steps.title: Facilitator pause: show one live revision loop
steps.goal: Model how to revise based on the gap, not just randomly add more words.
steps.facilitator_notes: Take one learner-safe example and revise it once for clarity and once for output format.

steps.title: Choose your pathway
steps.goal: Route learners into the right level without overload.

branch_rules.next_focus: compare outputs, verify claims, or extend complexity

steps.learner_prompt: Choose Guided, Build, Remix, or Stretch.

steps.title: Pathway work block
steps.goal: Give a right-sized task based on learner confidence and pace.

guided.task: Use a sentence frame to improve a prompt about planning, writing, or school support.
guided.sentence_frame: Help me create a ____ for a ____ audience. Keep it under ____ and show it as ____.

build.task: Create a practical prompt for something you would really use this month.

remix.task: Take one good answer and remake it for a different audience, tone, or format.

stretch.task: Ask the same question two different ways and compare what changed, what improved, and what still needs checking.

steps.title: Personal mini-project
steps.goal: Let learners apply the pattern to something personally relevant.
steps.learner_prompt: Create one prompt for a real interest: school, creativity, hobbies, planning, or useful life task. Save the best version and note one improvement you made.

steps.title: Wrap-up and takeaway
steps.goal: Capture learning and useful next steps.
steps.learner_prompt: What worked best for you tonight? What would you still verify? What is one prompt habit you want to keep using?
steps.facilitator_notes: End with one concrete takeaway, not a lecture.
